content.full_text:
David Pitman                                          October 28, 1976


Richard Eichman

Renewal of Registration


     As a result of our recent discussion at the interagency meeting
of October 14, 1976, the Commission staff has reviewed the require-
ments for renewal of registration (Section 86102) and has determined
the following:

     1. A lobbyist is considered registered until he/she files
        a Notice of Termination.

     2. A lobbyist who intends to retain his/her registration
        must renew the registration within twenty days after
        the opening of each regular session of the Legislature.

     3. A lobbyist who has not terminated or has not renewed
        his/her registration within twenty days after the open-
        ing of each regular session of the Legislature should
        be considered delinquent in the filing of the renewal
        registration.

     We suggest that Form 640 (Lobbyist Notice of Termination) be
included in the registration package you intend to send to registered
lobbyists. Lobbyist should be instructed to file either the Notice
of Termination or re-register within twenty days after the opening
of the regular session of the Legislature.


cc: Robert Summers
    Floyd Shimomura

bcc: Ken F., Bob S., Mickey, Natalie, Ted, John K., Nan, Gina,
     John Greenwood, Jeanne (Advice File)


State of California

Memorandum

To      :    John S. Keplinger                                    Date:  October 20, 1976
         Fair Political Practices Commission


From  :  Secretary of State


Subject:     Request for Interpretation of Lobbyist Termination


         This is to request an interpretation of the requirement to
terminate lobbyist registrations with regard to the effective
date when that date precedes submission of the notice of ter-
mination by 30 days.

         As evidenced by the two examples attached, on occasion we do
receive notices of termination indicating effective dates
substantially earlier than the 30 days noted on the date of
execution.  This office could correspond with each case and
clarify the appropriate date, or we could accept the earlier
date on its face.

         We are raising this question with you so that our actions might
conform with advise issued from your office.  It is apparent
that in some instances, lobbyists have stopped lobbying and,
after the fact, "realize" they must submit a notice of termi-
nation.  Our concern is with regard to lobbyist activities,
hence, any enforcement questions which might arise.


                                    [signature]
                                    DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
                                    Political Reform Division

DBP/cs
Att.


(Interim Form)
LOBBYIST
NOTICE OF TERMINATION

(GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 86103)

Form 64C

Paul K. Richardson
NAME OF LOBBYIST

Friends of the River
ADDRESS

have terminated all acti

requirin my registration under Title 9 of the California State Government Code effective July 1, 1926
DATE

I have [illegible] and understand that I shall remain subject to Sections 86202 and 86203 of Title 9 for six months after filing

terminat [illegible]

86202. It shall be unlawful for a lobbyist to make a contribution, or to act as an
agent or intermediary in the making of any contribution, or to arrange for the
making of any contribution by himself or by any other person.

"Contribution" as used in this article means a contribution made to a
state candidate, a committee supporting a state candidate, or an elected state
officer. (Government Code Section 86200)

86203. It shall be unlawful for a lobbyist to make gifts to one person aggregating
more than ten dollars ($10) in a calendar month, or to act as an intermediary in
the making of any gift by any other person.

"Gift" as used in this article means a gift made directly or indirectly to
a state candidate, an elected state officer, a legislative official or an agency
official. (Government Code Section 86201)

I declare [illegible] under penalty of perjury that this statement is true and correct to the best of my knowledge.

Executed [illegible] on Oct. 15 at Sacramento by Paul K. Richardson
DATE CITY AND STATE SIGNATURE OF LOBBYIST


FPPC
OCT 12 12:51PM'76

(Interim Form)
LOBBYIST
NOTICE OF TERMINATION
(GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 86103)

Form 640

I ___Scott E. Slotterbeck, II___
NAME OF LOBBYIST

of __2425 Oak #4, Sacramento, Calif___
ADDRESS

have terminated all activities

requiring my registration under Title 9 of the California State Government Code effective ___July 1, 1976___
DATE

I have read and understood that I shall remain subject to Sections 86202 and 86203 of Title 9 for six months after filing this termination.

86202. It shall be unlawful for a lobbyist to make a contribution, or to act as an agent or intermediary in the making of any contribution, or to arrange for the making of any contribution by himself or by any other person.

"Contribution" as used in this article means a contribution made to a state candidate, a committee supporting a state candidate, or an elected state officer. (Government Code Section 86200)

86203. It shall be unlawful for a lobbyist to make gifts to one person aggregating more than ten dollars ($10) in a calendar month, or to act as an intermediary in the making of any gift by any other person.

"Gift" as used in this article means a gift made directly or indirectly to a state candidate, an elected state officer, a legislative official or an agency official. (Government Code Section 86201)

I declare under penalty of perjury that this statement is true and correct to the best of my knowledge.

Executed on Oct 5, 1976 at Sacramento, CA by [signature]
DATE           CITY AND STATE        SIGNATURE OF LOBBYIST

RECEIVED
AND FILED
in the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California
OCT 13 1976

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY

A _______________

B _______________
